name: Filipino Breakfast Steaks
recipeIngredient: 2.25 pounds Sirloin, sliced to 1/8 to 1/4 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the salt, brown sugar, garlic, and black pepper.
2. Spread the rub evenly on both sides of the meat.
3. Store covered (or in a plastic bag) in the refrigerator overnight.
4. Sun dry for a couple of hours before cooking in a smoker to medium or medium well.
Serving Suggestions:
- Serve with a salsa made from chopped fresh tomatoes, chopped onions, grated radish, and chopped fresh cilantro.
- Add oriental fish sauce (or salt) to taste, crushed hot chiles, and a little vinegar (or lemon juice).
- For heavy eaters, chop the meat into small pieces and serve sprinkled over a mound of garlic fried rice and fried eggs.
